blocks.content: In this milestone, you test your configuration to verify that IRM creates an Alert group from a firing alert and executes escalation and notification steps as expected.

1. Trigger the alert rule to fire:
   1. In **Alerting** > **Alert rules**, locate the rule connected to your IRM contact point.
   1. Open the rule and click **Edit** to lower the conditions so the rule fires (for example, set CPU > `5%`).
   1. If necessary, lower the evaluation period to `1 minute` so it fires quickly.
   1. Save the rule, wait one evaluation cycle, and confirm the rule shows a firing state.
1. Verify the new Alert group in IRM:
   1. In the IRM navigation menu, click **Alert Groups**.
   1. Locate the new Alert group created by the firing rule.
   1. Open the Alert group details.
1. In the timeline, verify escalation chain selection, the correct on-call user identification, and first notification attempts.
1. If you configured wait and escalation steps, wait for the delay to elapse and verify the next steps fire.
1. When you're ready, acknowledge the Alert group to stop further escalation and notifications.
1. Restore the alert rule conditions and confirm it returns to a non-firing (OK) state after the next evaluation.
If you don't have an alert rule connected to IRM, you can run a partial test by sending a demo alert from the integration page.
Send a demo alert and view the resulting Alert group.

steps.tooltip: Open the integrations list and select your Grafana Alerting integration.
Trigger a demo alert. Review and optionally customize the payload.
Confirm and send the demo alert.
Open Alert groups to see the demo alert and its escalation timeline.

blocks.content:
When your test is successful, you observe the following:

- A new Alert group appears in **IRM** > **Alert Groups** within a few seconds of the alert firing.
- The Alert group timeline shows escalation chain selection, on-call user identification based on your schedule, notification attempts with timestamps, and escalation steps progressing on the configured delays.
- You receive notifications through your configured channels (email, mobile app, Slack, SMS) at the expected times.
- When you acknowledge the Alert group, the timeline shows the acknowledgment and all further escalation and notifications stop.

If your test doesn't produce the expected results, check the troubleshooting links at the bottom of this page.

In the final milestone, you review what you accomplished and plan next steps.
